2 POLE BARNS DOWN WHICH CONTAINED COWS...SMALL SHEDS DOWN. FENCES AND TREES KNOCKED DOWN. GUTTER TO HOME WAS DAMAGED. HALF MILE LONG DAMAGE PATH...APPROX 50-75 YARDS WIDE.

STRONG MICROBURST WINDS DAMAGED APPROX 150 TREES...5 OUTDOOR STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED OR COLLAPSED KILLING 7 COWS IN THE PROCESS...SEVERAL OTHER COWS WERE INJURED. MINOR DAMAGE TO 3 OR 4 HOMES. DAMAGE SWATH BEGINS 0.2 MILES SSW OF FARMERSVILLE AND EXTENDS NE TO KNOWLES...ROUGHLY A HALF MILE. THE WIDTH OF DAMAGE WAS NARROW...ONLY ABOUT 75 YARDS. THIS INFORMATION WAS GATHERED FROM A NWS STORM SURVEY CONDUCTED DURING THE AFTERNOON OF MAY 14.

PRELIM RATED AS AN EF0 WITH WINDS OF 65 MPH. MOST LIKELY A RAIN-WRAPPED TORNADO AT TIMES AND DIFFICULT TO SEE. A THANKS IS EXTENDED TO THE MUKWONAGO POLICE FOR THEIR HELP.

INTERSECTION OF GEBHARDT AND BARKER ROADS. 2.5 FEET OF WATER IN THE INTERSECTION WAS ESTIMATED VIA THE DEPTH IN RELATION TO STREET SIGNS. A VEHICLE WAS TRAPPED IN WATER UP TO THE DRIVERS WINDOW. BROOKFIELD FIRE DEPARTMENT RESPONDED TO RESCUE THE DRIVER.

A STORM SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR CLEAR LAKE...OR 5 SE OF EDGERTON. A SCREEN WAS FLIPPED OVER A TRAILER. MINIMAL TREE AND POWER LINE DAMAGE WAS ALSO OBSERVED MAINLY TO THE SOUTHEAST OF THE LAKE.

TORNADO TRACKED FROM 4.2 NW SHULLSBURG TO 2.0 NNE SHULLSBURG. PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT 4.5 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 100 YARDS. A BARN WAS DESTROYED NEAR THE BEGINNING OF THE TRACK WITH LARGE TREE LIMBS AND TREES SNAPPED. NEAR THE END OF THE TRACK A METAL SHED WAS DESTROYED...WITH SOME METAL FRAGMENTS BLOWN ABOUT 100 YARDS DOWNSTREAM. MAXIMUM WINDS PROBABLY PEAKED IN THE 100 TO 105 MPH RANGE...AT THE TOP OF THE EF-1 RANGE.
SHED BLOWN DOWN ON COUNTY HWY Q BETWEEN BELMONT AND SCHULLSBURG
BARN DAMAGE ON COUNTY HIGHWAY O NORTH OF SHULLSBURG.
BRANCHES REPORTED DOWN IN THE AREA AROUND THE SAME TIME.

BASEBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN THE SOUTHWEST PART OF LAFAYETTE COUNTY IN THE BENTON AREA WITH THE SECOND STORM THAT MOVED THROUGH. REPORTS OF HAIL DAMAGE TO CROPS...VEHICLES...AND BUILDINGS WERE ALSO RECEIVED FROM BOTH ROUNDS OF STORMS. THOUSANDS OF ACRES OF CORN AND SOYBEAN CROPS WERE DESTROYED. SNOW PLOWS WERE USED TO CLEAR THE ROADWAYS OF ACCUMULATING HAIL. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.

TORNADO TRACKED FROM 1.8 NE NEW DIGGINGS TO 3.6 S SHULLSBURG. PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT 7.5 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 75 YARDS. NEAR THE BEGINNING OF THE TRACK...A SMALL METAL SHED WAS DESTROYED WITH SOME NEARBY TREE LIMB DAMAGE. THE TORNADO THEN CAUSED SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE. NEAR THE END OF THE TRACK...A HOUSE HAD PART OF THE ROOF AND ITS WESTERN WALL RIPPED OFF AND WINDOWS ON THREE SIDES BLOWN IN. MAXIMUM WINDS PROBABLY PEAKED AROUND 90 MPH...AT THE BOTTOM OF THE EF-1 RANGE.

LOTS OF WATER OVER TWIN BRIDGE ROAD ABOUT 3 MILES NORTH OF VILLAGE OF NEW DIGGINGS. GALENA RIVER BASIN. ALSO LOTS OF HAIL.

TORNADO BEGAN ABOUT 2.9 SW OF MONTELLO IN MARQUETTE COUNTY AND TRACKED TO ABOUT 1.5 NW OF DALTON IN GREEN LAKE COUNTY...ENDING AROUND 616 PM. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 9 MILES WITH A MAX PATH WIDTH OF ABOUT 100 YARDS. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG THE ENTIRETY OF THE TRACK...WITH SOME UPROOTED TREES. MAX WINDS IN THE TORNADO WERE LIKELY AROUND 95 MPH...OR IN THE EF-1 RANGE. HAIL AND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WERE ALSO OBSERVED 1 TO 3 MILES EITHER SIDE OF THE TORNADO TRACK.

QUARTER SIZE HAIL FROM CAMBRIA UP TO HWY 33

LOTS OF GRAVEL...DIRT...AND DEBRIS ACROSS LOW SPOTS IN ROADS IN THE NORTHWEST PART OF THE COUNTY. SHOULDERS PARTIALLY ERODED AND WASHED OUT FROM FAST FLOWING WATER. SEVERAL LOCATIONS ALONG RURAL ROADS BETWEEN BLACKHAWK LAKE AND MUSCODA, INCLUDING PORTIONS OF HIGHWAY P. TIME ESTIMATED.

DAMAGING WIND SWATH FOUND FROM 4 MILES NORTH OF DARLINGTON THROUGH THE NORTHWEST SIDE OF THE CITY AND ABOUT 100 YARDS WIDE. WIND SWATH BEGAN AROUND 740 AND ENDED ABOUT 750 PM. CORN FLATTENED NORTH OF THE CITY AND SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED IN THE CITY PARK. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 75 MPH.
A FEW 2 TO 3 FOOT DIAMETER TREES WERE SNAPPED AND DOWN IN A CITY PARK IN DARLINGTON. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH SEVERE STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.

THIS TORNADO SPUN UP ABOUT 0.2 MILES SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF CHURCH ROAD AND HIGHWAY 78...OR ABOUT 2.6 MILES NORTHEAST OF GRATIOT. TREE LIMBS ABOUT 1 FOOT IN DIAMETER WERE BLOWN DOWN AND SMASHED THREE VEHICLES. SOME TREES WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN. THERE WAS MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO TWO FARM HOUSES. PATH WIDTH ABOUT 25 YARDS. PATH LENGTH ABOUT 0.1 MILES. BRIEF TORNADO. MAX WIND SPEED ABOUT 80 MPH...OR AT THE TOP END OF THE EF-0 RANGE.
GRAVEL...DIRT...AND DEBRIS ACROSS LOW SPOTS IN ROAD...FROM FAST RUNNING WATER. ALONG HIGHWAY G BETWEEN BELMONT AND CALAMINE. TIME ESTIMATED.

A LOT OF TREE AND POWERLINE DAMAGE FROM WATERFORD TO BURLINGTON BETWEEN 345 PM CDT AND 350 PM CDT.

A LARGE TREE FELL ONTO A HOUSE. LAW ENFORCEMENT ALSO REPORTS TREES...LIMBS...AND POWER LINES DOWN IN PRETTY MUCH EVERY TOWNSHIP IN THE COUNTY. THE MOST CONCENTRATED DAMAGE STRETCHES FROM WATERFORD AND WIND LAKE EAST TO NEAR CALEDONIA.
LARGE SECTION OF A CORN FIELD FLATTENED. LARGE TREE BLOWN DOWN...AND SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN.
12 INCH AND 10 INCH LIMBS DOWN.

SEVERAL REPORTS FROM THE AREA ABOUT 3 SW RACINE. A CAMPER WAS LIFTED UP AND SLAMMED ONTO A CAR IN THE HIGH WINDS. TREES WERE BENT SIDEWAYS AND LARGE LIMBS WERE DOWN. SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF SOME ROOFS NEAR THE WRJN RADIO STATION. LARGE LIMBS WERE DOWN ALONG TAYLOR AVENUE IN ELMWOOD PARK.
LARGE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN THE CITY OF BLANCHARDVILLE.

CONCENTRATED AREA OF WIND DAMAGE IN HOLLANDALE AND WALDWICK. MANY TREES WERE DOWN ACROSS THIS AREA. IN WALDWICK...CLOSE TO HALF OF THE PROPERTIES IN THE TOWN HAD LARGE LIMBS OR TREES DOWN. THERE WAS A TREE ON A HOUSE AND SHINGLES OFF SEVERAL ROOFS. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.

A CONCENTRATED AREA OF DAMAGE WAS REPORTED IN FAR SOUTHWEST DANE COUNTY ALONG BERG ROAD. ABOUT 20 TO 25 TREES WERE DOWNED ON ONE PROPERTY...SOME AS TALL AS 50 FEET. TREES AND POWER LINES WERE ALSO DOWN ON THE ROAD. RESIDENT REPORTED WIND SHIFTED SUDDENLY WITH BLINDING RAINFALL THAT HE DESCRIBED AS WHITEOUT CONDITIONS. DAMAGE WAS LIKELY THE RESULT OF A WET MICROBURST...AS ALL TREES WERE DOWNED FACING IN THE SAME DIRECTION.

REPORT RECEIVED FROM THE 2400 BLOCK OF SWAN BLVD IN WAUWATOSA. TWO DISTINCT SEVERE WIND GUSTS...ESTIMATED TO BE IN EXCESS OF 60 MPH. ONE PERSON HAPPENED TO SEE HER ROOF FLEX IN THE STRONG WIND. TWO 50 YEAR OLD TREES HAD LARGE LIMBS SNAPPED OFF AND THEIR TRUNKS CRACKED. ONE TREE SPLIT IN HALF. OTHER TREE DAMAGE ALSO REPORTED. THE GUSTS WERE UNUSUAL IN THAT THEY WERE AT THE TAIL END OF SOME HEAVIER RAIN...NOT AT THE LEADING EDGE OF THE STORMS. NO OBSERVATION SITES INDICATED A WAKE LOW.

DELAYED REPORT...DAMAGE TO A FEW TREES ON THE WEST SIDE OF LAKE PEWAUKEE. 2 FOOT DIAMETER TREE SPLIT IN HALF...TOP OF 20 FOOT PINE TREE SHEARED OFF...ANOTHER 1.5 DIAMETER TREE ALSO SPLIT DOWN THE MIDDLE. OTHER EVIDENCE OF BRANCHES BEING CLEANED OFF THE ROAD.
